chief_complaint: A 56-year-old Asian woman presented with acute chest pain following an intense emotional and physical altercation.
history: History of hypertension and diabetes. Presented with acute chest pain following an intense emotional and physical altercation. Hemodynamically stable on admission. Minor ST-segment elevation in the anterior leads and modest troponin rise on electrocardiogram.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not explicitly stated.
labs: Electrocardiogram: minor ST-segment elevation in the anterior leads. Troponin rise. Bedside echocardiography: apical akinesia. Coronary angiography: high-risk multivessel coronary artery disease, including significant left main disease.
correct_diagnosis: Takotsubo cardiomyopathy with rapid left ventricular recovery unmasking asymptomatic but significant coronary artery disease
differentials: Takotsubo cardiomyopathy with rapid left ventricular recovery unmasking asymptomatic but significant coronary artery disease
correct_ranking: Takotsubo cardiomyopathy with rapid left ventricular recovery unmasking asymptomatic but significant coronary artery disease
